meaning: n.加勒比人(南美洲东北部印第安人)
examples: Small genus of deciduous west Indian trees or shrubs: Carib wood.
The peoples of these shores are the Garifuna, a hbyrid through the marriage of African and Carib Indian.
The peoples of these shores are the Garifuna, a hybrid through the marriage of African and Carib Indian.
Once the home of Arawak and Carib peoples, it became an English colony after settlers arrived in 1650.
Visited by the explorer Christopher Columbus in 1493, who named it after a Spanish monastery, the territory was home to Carib indians who resisted Spanish attempts to settle the islands.
